title: wrong message about "use a ILP64 abi for larger tables"
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

In https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/60a787fa50062169c1aae4ecb199d13c0b6f25b6/kernel/hashlib.h#L191
```
	if (sizeof(int) == 4)
		throw std::length_error("hash table exceeded maximum size. use a ILP64 abi for larger tables.");
```
is not helpful.  ILP64 is quite dead and LP64 uses 4 byte ints.  If you want to allow larger values then you should use 'long long' not int as the type [long is 32bit on windows].    

A failure of this check is seen in https://github.com/The-OpenROAD-Project/OpenROAD/issues/2106

## Expected behavior

64 bit linux compiles (LP64) should allow for the larger table size.

## Actual behavior

64 bit linux compiles (LP64) don't allow for the larger table size and gives an unhelpful message.
code_blocks:
if (sizeof(int) == 4)
		throw std::length_error("hash table exceeded maximum size. use a ILP64 abi for larger tables.");
